differences: The bottom sheet title, description, row, divider, checkbox, and two-button footer now match the Wave1 picker structure.
The backdrop page remains the in-progress shopping detail state in MVP, while the fixed prototype reference shows the completed/read-only shopping state behind the sheet.
The sheet top edge is a few pixels lower in the generated 390px capture because the MVP completion flow opens the picker before the complete API call resolves.

classified_differences.difference: Backdrop is in-progress rather than completed/read-only
classified_differences.reason: MVP complete API requires the pantry reflection choice before marking the list completed, so the sheet cannot be opened from the completed read-only state without changing completion semantics.

suggestions: Keep the picker API semantics unchanged: all selected maps to null/default policy, none maps to [], subset maps to selected IDs.
If product later allows a two-step optimistic completion UI, refreeze the prototype/MVP flow together before changing the backdrop state.
reasoning: The mobile picker sheet itself is visually aligned with the fixed Wave1 reference. The remaining backdrop mismatch is classified as functional-contract-required because the production API needs the selection before completion.
